one_time_description: Nlaka'pamux Legacy Trust one-time receipt of $400,000 (no comparative amount in prior year)
economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Squianny Development Corporation (100%), Zakwaski Resources Limited Partnership (99.9%), and Squianny Limited Liability Partnership (99.9%), accounted for under modified equity method. Kwoiek Creek Resources Limited Partnership also referenced as revenue source. COVID-19 noted as having no material impact on operations.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) no budgeted information included in financial statements, and (2) insufficient audit evidence for GBE/GBP investments. The First Nation accounts for three entities under modified equity method: Squianny Development Corporation, Zakwaski Resources Limited Partnership, and Squianny Limited Liability Partnership. Funds held in Ottawa Trust Fund ($276,555) included as financial assets. Bank indebtedness of $100,877 included in current liabilities. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($720,676) + DFO ($28,000) + CMHC ($11,801) + Province of BC ($682,276) + FNESC ($16,979) + Union of BC Municipalities ($30,688) + New Relationship Trust ($0) + ASETS ($0) = $1,490,420 plus Nlaka'pamux Legacy Trust $400,000 = $1,890,420 — however Nlaka'pamux Legacy Trust and Union of BC Municipalities may not be government transfers; osr adjusted accordingly. Audit report dated February 10, 2023, well after the July 2021 filing deadline, noted as a violation of the First Nations Financial Transparency Act. No schedule of remuneration provided.
